moments.text: Notice of motion and we're going to go to the speakers on that Councillor Lavalette please.
moments.summary: Clerk accepts a notice of motion and calls speakers.

moments.text: High standards of behaviour, conduct and integrity. Personal comments are unacceptable.
moments.summary: Chair reminds councillors of standards and respectful debate.

moments.text: You should wait at assembly point C. Okay, moving straight along. Apologies,. Chief Executive to read. Thank you, Chair. We've received apologies from Councillor Crawford,.
moments.summary: Fire evacuation instructions and apologies read out.

moments.text: Sit down, please.
moments.summary: Chair instructing councillor to sit down during meeting.

moments.text: I'll SEND out an AXA.
moments.summary: Procedural mention of approving a Child and Youth Justice Plan.

moments.text: And reserve the right to speak later.
moments.summary: Routine procedural statement reserving right to speak later.

moments.text: With all the opposition groups. Conservative group through. Of opposition groups at Cabinet.
moments.summary: Routine confirmation of debate length on constitutional changes.

moments.text: You then say any notice of motion.
moments.summary: Pure procedural item with no political content.

moments.text: Approaches opposition.
moments.summary: Vague procedural statement with no substantive content.

moments.text: Councillors in the opposition. The Reform group. To the previous Labour. And Conservative administrations.
moments.summary: Procedural comment about opposition groups.

moments.text: Of this scrutiny process.
moments.summary: Vague procedural statement about scrutiny process.

moments.text: Conservative administration.
moments.summary: Procedural reference to previous administration.

moments.text: Report of the audit risk.
moments.summary: Purely procedural item with no political content.

moments.text: And members of the Audit, Risk.
moments.summary: Procedural mention of Audit, Risk and members without substantive content.

moments.text: I do apologise for reading a couple of notes from my laptop.
moments.speaker: Whipp
moments.summary: Councillor apologises for reading notes during meeting.

moments.text: Whether that will be an opposition member.
moments.summary: Unclear procedural comment with no identifiable speaker or topic.

moments.text: I do apologise, Mr Chairman.
moments.summary: Apology for interjection, no political substance.

moments.text: Scrutiny committee.
moments.summary: Mentions cabinet scrutiny without actionable content.

moments.text: Notice of motion one. Please put forward your notice of motion.
moments.summary: Standard procedural instruction to present notice of motion.

moments.text: Backed cross-party at the last budget.
moments.summary: Incomplete and unclear procedural statement with no identifiable speaker or policy content.

moments.text: And wanted to improve my education.
moments.summary: Personal anecdote with no clear policy or political relevance to the council.

moments.text: And in my capacity as a Reform counter-councillor.
moments.summary: Vague statement of personal determination as Reform councillor.

moments.text: As a Reform councillor.
moments.summary: Self-deprecating remark about past actions as Reform councillor.

moments.text: Thank you Chairman and apologies to Councillor Barnes.
moments.summary: Chairman directing debate to Councillor Buckley with apology.

moments.text: Notice of motion to. Your notice of motion.
moments.summary: Chairman moving to next item with time reference.

moments.text: And reserve my right to speak later.
moments.summary: Procedural remark reserving right to speak later, no substantive content.

moments.text: Apologize to anyone from Yorkshire.
moments.summary: Light-hearted apology to Yorkshire, no political substance.

moments.text: Scrutiny.
moments.summary: Scrutiny mentioned in passing with no substantive content.

moments.text: That it's quite appropriate to bring to scrutiny.
moments.summary: Vague procedural comment about technical issues being appropriate for scrutiny.

moments.text: Reform during the election.
moments.summary: Generic comment about listening to Reform UK during an election.

moments.text: So thanks to Reform.
moments.summary: Acknowledgment of reading through agenda items, thanking Reform UK.

moments.text: For Reform.
moments.summary: Procedural roll call response.

moments.text: Can I just raise a point of order.
moments.summary: Point of order raised but no substantive content provided.

moments.text: But as a point of order.
moments.summary: Another point of order with no meaningful information.

moments.text: So as a point of order.
moments.summary: Point of order raised during debate, no substantive issue disclosed.

moments.text: Notice of motion three. Councillor Dowding, please put forward your notice of motion.
moments.summary: Chair inviting councillor to present notice of motion.

moments.text: I all heartedly and the Liberal Democrat group all heartedly support the motion that's been.
moments.summary: Liberal Democrats support motion on electoral reform but cite outdated precedent.

moments.text: Your notice of motion thank you very much chairman I think this is something we could we.
moments.summary: Procedural vote on original motion with no debate content.
